question:
Will Elon Musk post 400-419 tweets from October 17 to October 24, 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from October 17 12:00 PM ET to October 24, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

evidences.title: Profiling Elon Musk's Twitter/X evolution: Political Thought, Public ...
evidences.content: ## Description This report examines the evolution of Elon Musk’s Twitter (now X) presence from 2010 to early 2025, analyzing his posting behavior, ideological shifts, emotional tone, and influence on public discourse. We used advanced computational text analysis techniques, including emotion detection, personality profiling (Big Five model), and political ideology classification. We traced how Musk's communication style transformed from predominantly neutral and technology-centric discourse into a more politically engaged and emotionally charged presence. The study highlights key trends, such as increased ideologically polarized content, heightened engagement patterns for controversial posts, and shifts in personality traits correlated with significant real-world events. [...] The report comes with two datasets in CSV files: Dataset 1: The Complete Musk Tweets (Emotion and Personality Annotation) [The Complete Musk\_comprehensive (Emotion and Personality Annotation).csv] This dataset contains 60,567 tweets from Elon Musk, spanning from June 4, 2010, to January 24, 2025. Each tweet has been annotated with emotion and personality traits using a machine learning algorithm. The data...

evidences.title: Echoes of Power: Elon Musk, Misinformation, and the Fragility of Democratic Discourse | TechPolicy.Press
evidences.content: This influence doesn’t end with Musk’s posts. A BuzzSumo analysis conducted on April 8, 2025, shows that even secondary reporting on Musk’s sarcastic reactions goes viral. One example is a New York Post story headlined “Elon Musk gloats as X fact-checks Biden tweet that ‘no one is above the law’ after Hunter pardon,” which generated significant traction across platforms, with a dominant Facebook reaction of laughter. Despite being just one of many stories covering Musk’s post, the article drew over 1.4K engagements, indicating how Musk’s narrative cues cascade across the media ecosystem and are rewarded with emotional amplification—in this case, mockery and ridicule. His digital rhetoric doesn’t just spread; it sets the tone for how others frame, interpret, and spread it further. [...] Figure 1: Engagement metrics for New York Post coverage of Musk’s Hunter Biden reaction (BuzzSumo, April 8, 2025). Note: The BuzzSumo screenshot shows total engagements (1.4K) and dominant laughter reaction for a New York Post story on Elon Musk’s sarcastic tweet following President Biden’s pardon of his son. This reflects secondary narrative amplification through media reporting and public emotio...

evidences.content: # Even with obstacles, Elon Musk’s X will see ad sales grow, says Emarketer ## Bloomberg Technology 729000 subscribers 28 likes ### Description 1388 views Posted: 26 Mar 2025 Jasmine Enberg, EMARKETER Principal Analyst says X is on track for its first year of ad sales gains since Elon Musk bought the social media platform. She explains what’s behind the increase with Caroline Hyde on “Bloomberg Technology”. [...] recovery, not a rebound. Even with the growth that we are projecting, XS ad business in 2025 is still going to be smaller than Twitter's ad business was back in 2019. And to your question, some of this growth is actually being driven by fear. There are plenty of advertisers out there now who see spending on access, kind of a cost of doing business in order to mitigate any potential legal or financial repercussions. The problem, of course, is that fear is not a sustainable motivator. Luckily for X, it's not just fear that's driving this growth. It has also been able to secure a new base of advertisers, including small and medium sized businesses, which is something that Twitter historically struggled with. The problem there is that these [...] struggled with. The problem...

evidences.title: X's audience shifts right without losing reach
evidences.content: X has seen a shift to the right in its audience, without losing overall reach, according to our Digital News Report 2025. Here are three things to know about this issue: 1️⃣ Usage of X for news is stable or increasing across many markets. The biggest uplift is in the United States (+8 points for news from last year to 23%), Australia and Poland ( both +6). 2️⃣ Since Elon Musk bought the social network in 2022, many more right-leaning people, notably young men, have flocked to it, while some progressive audiences have left or are using it less frequently. Twitter pre-2022 was populated mostly by those on the progressive side of the political spectrum, but the proportion that self-identifies on the right has grown after Elon Musk took over. 3️⃣ In the US, last year’s presidential election [...] After two years of chaos at Twitter, which alternative has actually lasted? Hint: One of them just passed 30 million users. Two years after Elon Musk bought Twitter (now X), plenty of alternatives have come and gone. Bluesky is the one that has stuck around. In early 2025, it crossed 30 million users. That is far from Meta’s Threads with 275 million, but enough to make it the strongest cont...

For all figures except those that explicitly require timestamps and are sensitive to exact hour/time, I convert these timestamps to CST. Ultimately, I was able to gather data from 7/27/24 to 9/12/24 (48 days). #### 2 thoughts on “Elon Musk definitely tweets too much” 1. Image 10 ArcosJanuary 7, 2025 at 6:13 pm Reply Nice work! evidences.title: Twitter under Elon Musk
evidences.content: A computational analysis published on November 1, 2024, found potential algorithmic bias on X in favour of Republican Party "Republican Party (United States)") supporters. The study, which looked at more than 56,000 posts, identified a system-wide "structural break" on the platform around July 13, 2024, a date which coincides with Elon Musk's endorsement of Donald Trump in the 2024 US presidential election. From that point on, Elon Musk and other Republican commentators saw increased visibility for their posts. According to the researchers behind the study, this "could imply an algorithmic adjustment". On January 17, 2025, the European Commission requested to see X's internal documentation about its algorithms "and any recent changes made to it", following accusations of manipulation [...] 62. ↑ Cecil, Nicholas (August 5, 2024). evidences.content: ## Results ### General Tweet Characteristics and Engagement Patterns #### Tweet Volume and Frequency Analysis of the 2,391 tweets reveals distinct posting behaviors between Donald Trump and Elon Musk. Trump demonstrated a higher frequency of Twitter/X/X communication, averaging 7.3 tweets per day during active periods, compared to Musk's 3.8 tweets per day (table 2). Trump's tweeting pattern showed pronounced spikes during political events and controversies, while Musk's posting frequency remained more consistent with occasional surges around product launches and company announcements. evidences.title: How Traders Are Making Thousands Counting Elon Musk's ...
evidences.content: There's a market on Poly Market right now with $23 million in volume, $15 million traded in the last 24 hours. And it's not about war. It's not about an election. It's not even about crypto. It's about how many times Elon Musk is tweeting this week. 30 different brackets, 0 to 19 tweets, 20 to 39 all the way up to 580 plus. Thousands of people spending real money trying to predict the posting habits of one man. And somehow this might be the smartest market on the entire platform. Let me explain. So here's how it works. Every week, Poly Market opens a new market. How many times will Elon Musk post on X from Monday to Monday? Posts, quote tweets, and reposts all count. Replies do not, unless they show up on his main feed. The market splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. [...] splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. You buy the bracket you think he lands in, and if you're right, your share pays out a dollar. If you're wrong, they go to zero. Right now, the current week, February 24th to March 3rd, the market is pricing 300 to 319 tweets as the most likely outcome at 24%. The bracket below it, 280 to 299 is 22% and 320 to 339 is also 22%. So the m...

evidences.title: By the Numbers: How CEOs are using social media - PR Daily
evidences.content: At the other end of the spectrum, CEOs are shying away from X. Not only are CEOs posting 14 times less than they did in 2022, but their followings also shrank by 17% in the last two years. Since that time, X has seen massive upheaval with the purchase of the site by Elon Musk and has consistently struggled with brand safety. The number of CEOs who have claimed their names on X but never posted is up 75% since 2022. Among those CEOs who do still use the platform, they posted an average of six times per month, indicating the higher volume, more frequent cadence expected by most X users. What gets the most engagement

evidences.title: Elon Musk Runs X, Tesla, SpaceX, DOGE: Here's How He Got Here - Business Insider
evidences.content: Neuralink aims to treat patients with neurological conditions, such as paralysis and blindness. Initially, in the first human trial, Neuralink's goal is to help patients with paralyzed limbs control devices like a computer mouse or keyboard with only their thoughts. Noland Arbaugh became the first person to receive Neuralink's brain-chip implant in January 2024. The 29-year-old quadriplegic told Business Insider it had helped him regain independence and reconnect socially. Musk has said he wants Neuralink to ultimately help humans achieve "symbiosis" with artificial intelligence so that they don't get "left behind" as AI evolves over time. ## xAI [...] ## SpaceX SpaceX is a national and global leader in space exploration, and Musk's ultimate goal is to bring humans to Mars. Throughout the last 20 years, the company has broken record after record, including the most number of launches of a single rocket in one year. Beyond its rockets, SpaceX has built a wide satellite network and its global internet traffic more than tripled in 2024. The Starlink satellite internet system operates in Earth's lower orbit, with a constellation of up to 42,000 planned. [...] Starlink has become a k...

evidences.title: Your Guide to Social Media Marketing in 2025
evidences.content: June 17, 2025• 20MIN READ Authors Aishwarya Suresh Aishwarya Suresh Content WriterKarishma Borkakoty Karishma Borkakoty Content Marketer Let’s start this social media marketing guide with a small story. Who doesn’t know Tesla, the electric vehicle (EV) manufacturer led by visionary entrepreneur Elon Musk? Tesla keeps generating buzz by teasing upcoming products and features. Their “Battery Day” and “Cybertruck” unveilings are social media spectacles. During the Cybertruck unveiling event in 2019, Elon Musk demonstrated the truck’s “unbreakable” windows by having a Tesla designer throw a metal ball at the window. To everyone’s surprise, the window shattered! The awkward moment became an instant meme, flooding social media. [...] But here’s the twist: Despite the mishap, the Cybertruck pre-orders skyrocketed. Why? Because the incident showcased Tesla’s authenticity — live demos can go wrong, but Tesla owned it. Musk’s tweet afterward — “We’ll fix it in post”—turned a blunder into a viral moment. Remember, even broken windows can’t break a brand if handled with humor and transparency! This story is a testament to how powerful social media marketing (or SMM) is. It's not just about...

evidences.title: Elon Musk's X / Twitter is getting rid of repost, like, and ...
evidences.content: The most recent big change to the platform occurred just last month when Musk made most "likes" on the platform private. Previously, users could click the "like count" and see a list of users who liked a post. Now, only the user who published the post could see which users liked it. Third-parties can no longer see who liked what. Now, with Musk confirming the removal of repost and like buttons from posts, he will institute another change he's long wanted to make. "Next, we'll remove all the action buttons with their superfluous interaction counts from the main timeline," Musk posted") in a subscriber-only post on X in October of last year. "Just view count will show, unless you tap into a post." [...] "Yeah," replied") Musk. Topics Social Media X/Twitter Elon Musk [...] # Coming redesign of Elon Musk's X will get rid of... a lot Elon Musk and X logo Another big change that no one asked for is coming to X, the platform formerly known as Twitter. X is planning to redesign") how content shows up in users' feeds by removing interaction and engagement buttons from posts. evidences.title: Musk Tweet Tracker — Polymarket Automation - GitHub
evidences.content: # Musk Tweet Tracker — Polymarket Automation Automated toolkit for trading Elon Musk tweet count markets on Polymarket. Fetches live data from xtracker.io (official resolution source) and Polymarket API, analyzes tweet patterns, forecasts totals, and builds optimal position structures. ## How It Works Polymarket hosts prediction markets on how many times Musk will tweet over a given period (2-day and 7-day windows). The market is split into ranges (e.g. "201-225 tweets"). You build a structure of positions across ranges — if the actual count lands within your coverage, you profit. This toolkit automates the analysis: ## Project Structure [...] This toolkit automates the analysis: ## Project Structure `config.py — Configuration and constants polymarket_client.py — Polymarket API client (Gamma + CLOB) tweet_tracker.py — xtracker.io scraper with local history analyzer.py — Statistical analysis: rolling avg, wave detection, forecast position_builder.py — Position structure optimizer trade_logger.py — CSV trade journal with analytics main.py — CLI dashboard tying everything together` ## Quick Start ### Modes ## Strategy Rules (encoded in config) ## API Reference | API | Base URL | Au...

evidences.title: Elon Musk sets new date for Tesla's Robotaxi reveal | Mashable
evidences.content: # Elon Musk sets new date for Tesla's Robotaxi reveal Stan Schroeder Elon Musk August is out, October is in. Tesla CEO Elon Musk has set a new date for the company's big Robotaxi reveal: October 10. During an earnings call Tuesday, Musk said that moving the event back a few months allowed the company to "improve the Robotaxi, as well as add in a couple other things for the product unveil." The original launch date (after years of teasing) was August 8, as announced by Musk in April. A recent Bloomberg report, however, broke the news that the event was postponed for October, allegedly to give the teams working on Robotaxis more time to build additional prototypes. Earlier this month, Musk revealed that the event was delayed as he requested") an "important design change to the front." [...] Musk has been hailing Robotaxis as an extremely important next step for Tesla. The idea, according to Musk, is to build a fleet of fully autonomous cars which will ferry passengers around without the need for a driver. During yesterday's earnings call, Musk once again reiterated his belief that autonomous driving is key for Tesla's future. "The value of Tesla overwhelmingly is autonomy. These o...

[...] The Court of Chancery ruling striking down Musk’s pay has become a rallying cry for Delaware critics. Chancellor Kathaleen McCormick ruled that the Tesla board lacked independence from Musk when it approved the pay package in 2018 and that shareholders lacked key information when they voted overwhelmingly in favour of it. As a result, she applied a demanding legal standard and found the pay unfair to investors. Musk did not attend the arguments, which were held in a special court to accommodate the 65 people in attendance, mostly lawyers. evidences.title: Questions about how AI integration could affect X users
evidences.content: It is unclear what immediate changes X users might see from deeper AI integration. X data was already being used to train models, and a chatbot was already integrated into the platform. Possible effects on user policies, platform features, moderation, content governance, and regulatory scrutiny were not specified.
